steps.title: Locate the main verb
steps.content: The relative clause already has its main verb later: "encourages." The blank should describe Craciun's work without creating another finite verb that competes with that main predicate.

steps.title: Avoid a run-on clause
steps.content: Choice A, Choice C, and Choice D all create finite verb phrases before "encourages," producing two unjoined predicates in the same clause. A participle can modify "work" while leaving "encourages" as the clause's only main verb. The answer is B.
